Pose.safety: Maintain a neutral spine throughout. Keep your chest up and knees tracking over your toes. Do not let your knees collapse inward. If you feel knee pain, reduce range of motion.
Pose.Name: Bodyweight Squat
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury (severe), lower back herniation, hip impingement

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed outward. Keep your chest up and core braced.
Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering your body as if sitting in a chair. Keep your weight on your heels.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as far as comfortable). Keep your back straight and chest lifted.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to return to the starting position. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
If you have trouble with balance, hold onto a wall or sturdy object. Focus on pushing your knees outward slightly to protect them.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting knees cave inward during descent or ascent.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Do not lock out your knees at the top of the movement. Control the weight on both the press and the return.
Pose.Name: Leg Press (Light)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, knee injury (severe), hip impingement

steps.step: Sit on the leg press machine with your lower back pressed firmly against the pad. Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Inhale as you slowly lower the platform by bending your knees, bringing them toward your chest. Stop when your knees are at 90 degrees.
Exhale as you press through your heels to extend your legs, returning the platform to the starting position. Do not lock your knees.
Perform 2 sets of 12 reps. Keep your glutes and lower back pressed against the seat throughout.
Keep your feet flat on the platform. Do not let your heels lift. Higher foot placement targets glutes; lower foot placement targets quads.

common_mistakes.mistake: Lifting lower back off the seat.
Locking knees at the top of the movement.
Using momentum to press the weight.

Pose.safety: Keep your hips pressed firmly into the pad. Avoid jerking the weight—control the movement. Do not hyperextend your knees at the top.
Pose.Name: Lying Leg Curl
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Hamstring tear, lower back injury, knee tendinitis

steps.step: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with your knees just off the edge of the pad. Place your heels under the roller pads.
Exhale as you curl your heels toward your glutes by bending your knees. Keep your hips pressed into the pad.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale as you lower with control back to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Do not let the weight stack touch between reps. Keep constant tension on your hamstrings throughout the set.

common_mistakes.mistake: Lifting hips off the pad during the curl.
Using momentum to curl the weight.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and lower back neutral. Avoid arching your back. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Pose.Name: Glute Bridge

steps.step: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Place your arms at your sides, palms down.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, keeping your body in a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Inhale as you lower your hips back to the floor with control. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Do not overextend your lower back at the top—the movement comes from your glutes, not your spine.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.
Lifting too high, straining the lower back.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and ribs down. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glutes forcefully at the top of the movement.
Pose.Name: Hip Thrust (Bodyweight)

steps.step: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench. Place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Roll your shoulders onto the bench.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes as hard as possible at the top.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top. Your body should form a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Inhale as you lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your chin tucked slightly to maintain a neutral spine. Think about pushing your hips toward the ceiling, not arching your back.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.
Using lower back to lift instead of glutes.
Letting feet move or slide during the thrust.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and back flat. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glutes at the top of each kick.
Pose.Name: Donkey Kick

steps.step: Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position. Place your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Exhale as you lift your right knee off the floor, keeping your knee bent at 90 degrees. Press your heel toward the ceiling.
At the top, squeeze your glute as hard as possible. Keep your core engaged to prevent lower back arching.
Inhale as you lower your knee back to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps per leg.
Imagine you're holding a cup of water on your lower back—don't let it spill. This keeps your core engaged and prevents arching.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back during the kick.
Kicking too high, losing glute engagement.
Using momentum to swing the leg.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged for balance. Use a wall or sturdy object for support. Move through a full range of motion—rise as high as possible, then lower until you feel a stretch.
Pose.Name: Standing Calf Raise
Pose.Equipment: Wall or Sturdy Object for Support
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Achilles tendinitis, plantar fasciitis, ankle injury

steps.step: Stand with your feet hip-width apart. Hold onto a wall or sturdy object for balance. Place the balls of your feet on the edge of a step or elevated surface (optional).
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Squeeze your calves at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top, feeling the contraction in your calves.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels back down, feeling a stretch in your calves. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Point your toes slightly outward to target the inner calf, or slightly inward to target the outer calf. For a deeper stretch, stand on a step.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing at the bottom of the movement.
Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your knees bent at a consistent angle throughout. Do not use your hands to push down on your knees. Move through a full range of motion.
Pose.Name: Seated Calf Raise
Pose.Equipment: Seated Calf Raise Machine or Dumbbell on Knee

steps.step: Sit on a bench or chair. Place the balls of your feet on a step or elevated surface. Rest a dumbbell on your knees (or use a seated calf raise machine).
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible, keeping your knees bent at a consistent angle.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your lower calves (soleus).
Inhale as you lower your heels down, feeling a deep stretch in the soleus. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
The seated position targets the soleus (lower calf), which is important for endurance and walking. Keep the movement slow and controlled.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using hands to push down on knees.
Not going through full range of motion.
Letting heels rest at the bottom (keep tension).

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Hold the dumbbell close to your chest. Do not let your knees cave inward. Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Pose.Name: Goblet Squat

steps.step: Hold a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands, cupping the top. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out.
Inhale as you push your hips back and lower into a squat, keeping your chest up and the dumbbell close to your body.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as far as comfortable). Keep your weight on your heels.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
The goblet position helps keep your chest upright, making it easier to maintain proper squat form. Use your elbows to push your knees outward if needed.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Do not lock your knees at the top. Control the weight on both the press and the return.
Pose.Name: Leg Press

steps.step: Sit on the leg press machine with your lower back pressed firmly against the pad. Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Inhale as you slowly lower the platform, bringing your knees toward your chest. Stop when your knees are at 90 degrees.
Exhale as you press through your heels to extend your legs. Do not lock your knees at the top.
Perform 3 sets of 12 reps. Keep your glutes pressed against the seat throughout.
Do not let the weight stack touch between reps. Keep constant tension on your quads for better muscle growth.

common_mistakes.mistake: Lifting lower back off the seat.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Do not lock your knees at the top. Control the weight on both the extension and the return.
Pose.Name: Leg Extension
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee tendinitis, patellofemoral pain, lower back injury

steps.step: Sit on the leg extension machine with your lower back pressed against the pad. Place your ankles behind the roller pads.
Exhale as you extend your legs until they are straight (but not locked). Squeeze your quads at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your quadriceps.
Inhale as you lower with control back to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Point your toes slightly upward (dorsiflexion) during the extension to increase quad activation. Do not let the weight stack touch between reps.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the weight.
Lifting lower back off the seat.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips, not your lower back. Maintain a slight bend in your knees throughout. Avoid rounding your back.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Light)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, hamstring tear, sciatica

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding dumbbells or a barbell in front of your thighs. Keep a slight bend in your knees.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the weight down your shins. Keep your back straight and chest up.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (usually just below the knees). Do not round your back.
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Imagine you're closing a car door with your glutes. This helps initiate the movement from your hips, not your lower back.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the knees too much (turning into a squat).
Looking down (keep gaze forward or slightly down).
Using lower back to lift instead of hamstrings.

Pose.safety: Keep your hips pressed into the pad. Avoid jerking the weight. Do not lock your knees at the bottom. Control the movement throughout.
Pose.Name: Lying Leg Curl

steps.step: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with your knees just off the edge of the pad. Place your heels under the roller pads.
Exhale as you curl your heels toward your glutes. Keep your hips pressed firmly into the pad.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale as you lower with control back to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Point your toes (plantarflexion) during the curl to increase hamstring activation. Do not let the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and lower back neutral. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Avoid arching your lower back.
Pose.Name: Glute Bridge
Pose.Equipment: Exercise Mat, Optional: Dumbbell on Hips

steps.step: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Place a dumbbell on your hips if desired.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your glutes as hard as possible.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
For added difficulty, perform single-leg glute bridges by extending one leg straight while lifting with the other.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and ribs down. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glutes forcefully at the top. Use a pad on the barbell for comfort.
Pose.Name: Hip Thrust (Barbell, Light)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light Plates, Bench, Hip Thrust Pad (Optional)

steps.step: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench. Roll a barbell over your hips. Place a pad or towel under the bar for comfort.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top. Your body should form a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Keep your chin tucked slightly. Think about pushing your hips toward the ceiling, not arching your back. A pad on the bar makes the exercise much more comfortable.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.
Letting the barbell roll toward the stomach or thighs.
Using lower back to lift instead of glutes.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Push your knees outward as you squat to protect your knees. Do not let your knees cave inward.
Pose.Name: Sumo Squat
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury, hip impingement, lower back injury

steps.step: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outward at 45 degrees. Hold a dumbbell vertically in front of your body (optional).
Inhale as you push your hips back and lower into a squat, keeping your chest up. Push your knees outward to track over your toes.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as far as comfortable).
Exhale as you drive through your heels to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
The wide stance targets the glutes and inner thighs more than a traditional squat. Keep your chest up and knees pushed outward throughout.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and back flat. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glute at the peak of the kick. Do not use momentum to swing your leg.
Pose.Name: Cable Kickback

steps.step: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable pulley. Attach the strap to your right ankle. Stand facing the cable machine, holding the frame for support.
Exhale as you kick your right leg straight back, keeping your knee slightly bent. Squeeze your glute at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top, feeling your glute contract.
Inhale as you return to the starting position with control. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps per leg.
Do not lean forward during the kick. Keep your torso as still as possible—only your leg should move.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning forward to get more range of motion.
Using lower back to swing the leg.
Locking the knee at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and stand tall. Use a full range of motion. Do not bounce at the bottom. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Standing Calf Raise (Weighted)
Pose.Equipment: Dumbbell or Barbell, Step or Elevated Surface

steps.step: Stand with the balls of your feet on a step, heels hanging off. Hold a dumbbell in one hand or a barbell across your shoulders. Hold a wall for balance.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Squeeze your calves at the top.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, feeling the contraction.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels below the step, feeling a deep stretch in your calves. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
The deep stretch at the bottom is crucial for calf growth. Go as low as comfortably possible, but don't bounce.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing at the bottom of the movement.
Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your knees bent at a consistent angle. Do not use your hands to push down. Control the weight throughout.
Pose.Name: Seated Calf Raise (Weighted)
Pose.Equipment: Seated Calf Raise Machine or Barbell on Knees

steps.step: Sit on a seated calf raise machine or bench with a barbell across your knees. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible, keeping your knees bent at a consistent angle.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your soleus.
Inhale as you lower your heels down, feeling a deep stretch. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
The seated position isolates the soleus. This muscle responds well to higher reps (15-20) and slow, controlled movement.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using hands to push down on knees.
Not going through full range of motion.
Letting heels rest at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your legs straight (but not locked) on the leg press. Use a full range of motion. Do not let the weight stack slam at the bottom.
Pose.Name: Calf Press on Leg Press

steps.step: Sit on the leg press machine. Place the balls of your feet on the bottom edge of the platform, heels hanging off. Keep your legs straight (but not locked).
Exhale as you press the platform away by rising onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you lower your heels below the platform edge, feeling a stretch. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
This variation allows you to use heavier weight safely. Keep your legs straight throughout—don't use your quads to help.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending and straightening the knees (using quads).
Not going through full range of motion.
Slamming the weight at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Do not round your lower back. Use a squat rack with safety bars. Maintain proper depth (thighs parallel or below).
Pose.Name: Barbell Back Squat
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Quadriceps (Primary), Glutes, Hamstrings, Core, Lower Back
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, knee injury, hip impingement, shoulder mobility issues

steps.step: Place the barbell across your upper back (not your neck). Step under the bar, grip it wider than shoulders. Unrack with a deep breath.
Inhale and brace your core. Push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat. Keep your chest up.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or below. Keep your weight on your mid-foot.
Exhale as you drive through your feet to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Squeeze your upper back to create a shelf for the bar. Keep your elbows pointing down, not back. Use a belt for heavy sets.

common_mistakes.mistake: Rounding the lower back at the bottom.
Looking up or down (keep neck neutral).

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Do not round your lower back. Maintain an upright torso throughout. Use a squat rack with safety bars.
Pose.Name: Leg Press (Heavy)

steps.step: Sit on the leg press machine with your lower back pressed firmly against the pad. Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Inhale as you slowly lower the platform, bringing your knees toward your chest. Control the weight—do not let it crash.
Exhale as you press through your heels to extend your legs. Do not lock your knees.
Perform 4 sets of 10 reps. Do not let the weight stack touch between reps.
Heavy leg press requires controlled negatives. Lower the weight over 3-4 seconds, press explosively. This builds both strength and muscle.

common_mistakes.mistake: Lifting lower back off the seat.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Control the weight on both extension and return. Do not lock your knees at the top.
Pose.Name: Leg Extension

steps.step: Sit on the leg extension machine with your lower back pressed against the pad. Place your ankles behind the roller pads.
Exhale as you extend your legs until they are straight. Squeeze your quads at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your quadriceps as hard as possible.
Inhale as you lower with control over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Point your toes upward (dorsiflexion) during the extension to emphasize the rectus femoris. Slow negatives build serious quad mass.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the weight.
Lifting lower back off the seat.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Do not let your front knee travel past your toes. Control the movement on both the descent and ascent.
Pose.Name: Bulgarian Split Squat
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury, hip impingement, lower back injury

steps.step: Stand about 2 feet in front of a bench. Place the top of your back foot on the bench behind you. Hold dumbbells at your sides.
Inhale as you lower your back knee toward the floor, keeping your front heel planted and chest up.
Lower until your front thigh is parallel to the floor (or as far as comfortable).
Exhale as you drive through your front heel to return to standing. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps per leg.
This is a demanding exercise. Start with just bodyweight to master the balance, then add weight gradually. Keep your torso upright throughout.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting front knee travel past toes.
Lifting front heel off the floor.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips, not your lower back. Maintain a slight bend in your knees throughout. Do not round your back.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Heavy)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, hamstring tear, sciatica

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs. Keep a slight bend in your knees and core braced.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the bar down your shins. Keep your back straight and chest up.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (typically mid-shin level). Do not round your back.
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Think about pushing your hips back to close a car door. The bar should travel in a straight line up and down, close to your legs.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using lower back to lift instead of hamstrings.

Pose.safety: Keep your hips pressed into the pad. Control the weight—do not let it slam. Do not lock your knees at the bottom.
Pose.Name: Lying Leg Curl

steps.step: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with your knees just off the edge of the pad. Place your heels under the roller pads.
Exhale as you curl your heels toward your glutes. Keep your hips pressed into the pad.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your hamstrings as hard as possible.
Inhale as you lower with control over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Point your toes during the curl (plantarflexion) for better hamstring activation. Do not let the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: This is a demanding exercise. Use a pad under your knees for comfort. Control the descent—do not drop. Use a partner or anchor for stability.
Pose.Name: Nordic Curl
Pose.Equipment: Nordic Bench or Partner, Pad for Knees
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Hamstring tear (acute), lower back injury, knee tendinitis

steps.step: Kneel on a pad with your ankles secured under a Nordic bench or held by a partner. Keep your body straight from knees to head.
Inhale as you slowly lean forward, keeping your body straight. Resist the fall with your hamstrings as long as possible.
Lower until you can no longer resist, then catch yourself with your hands. Do not let your body slam.
Push yourself back up to the starting position with your hands. Perform 3 sets of 6 reps.
Nordic curls are extremely challenging. Most people cannot do a full rep—start with partial range of motion (lower only halfway) and progress over weeks.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting hips bend (breaking at the waist).
Not using a pad under knees.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Control the weight throughout. Do not lock your knees at the bottom.
Pose.Name: Seated Leg Curl

steps.step: Sit on the seated leg curl machine with your lower back pressed against the pad. Place your ankles behind the roller pads.
Exhale as you curl the weight toward your glutes, keeping your hips pressed into the seat.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale as you lower with control over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
The seated position targets the short head of the hamstring differently than lying curls. Use both variations for complete development.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and ribs down. Squeeze your glutes forcefully at the top. Use a pad on the barbell for comfort. Do not arch your lower back.
Pose.Name: Barbell Hip Thrust (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Bench, Hip Thrust Pad

steps.step: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench. Roll a heavy barbell over your hips. Use a pad for comfort.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes as hard as possible.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your glutes. Your body should form a straight line.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Heavy hip thrusts require full glute engagement. Think about pushing your hips toward the ceiling, not arching your back. Keep your chin tucked.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Maintain a slight bend in your knees throughout. Do not round your back. Push through your heels.
Pose.Name: Sumo Deadlift
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Glutes (Primary), Adductors (Inner Thighs), Hamstrings, Quadriceps, Lower Back
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, groin pull, knee injury

steps.step: Stand with feet wide (outside shoulder width), toes pointed outward. Grip the bar inside your knees, arms straight.
Inhale and brace your core. Drive through your heels to stand up, keeping your back straight. Push your knees outward.
At the top, squeeze your glutes and stand tall. Do not hyperextend your back.
Inhale as you lower the bar with control back to the floor. Perform 3 sets of 8 reps.
Sumo deadlifts emphasize the glutes and inner thighs. Keep your chest up and your knees pushed outward throughout the lift.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with the arms instead of driving with legs.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and back flat. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glute at the peak of the kick. Control the movement—do not swing.
Pose.Name: Cable Kickback

steps.step: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable pulley. Attach to your ankle. Stand facing the machine, holding the frame for support.
Exhale as you kick your leg straight back, keeping a slight bend in your knee. Squeeze your glute at the top.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your glute as hard as possible.
Inhale as you return with control over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps per leg.
Do not lean forward during the kick. Keep your torso as still as possible—only your leg should move. The slow return builds eccentric strength.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning forward to get more range.
Locking the knee at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Take controlled steps—do not rush. Do not let your front knee travel past your toes.
Pose.Name: Walking Lunge
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury, hip impingement, balance issues

steps.step: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides or a barbell across your back. Step forward with your right leg.
Inhale as you lower your back knee toward the floor, keeping your front heel planted and chest up.
Lower until your front thigh is parallel to the floor and your back knee hovers just above the ground.
Exhale as you drive through your front heel to stand up, bringing your back foot forward to take the next step. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps per leg.
Walking lunges are more functional than stationary lunges. Keep your torso upright and take controlled steps—don't rush.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting front knee travel past toes.
Letting back knee slam the floor.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and stand tall. Use a full range of motion. Do not bounce at the bottom. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Standing Calf Raise (Weighted - Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Standing Calf Raise Machine or Heavy Barbell with Step

steps.step: Stand on a standing calf raise machine or with a heavy barbell across your shoulders. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Squeeze your calves at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels below the step over 3 seconds, feeling a deep stretch. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
Calves respond well to heavy weight and slow negatives. Lower over 3-4 seconds, pause at the bottom, then explode up. No bouncing.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your knees bent at a consistent angle. Do not use your hands to push down. Control the weight throughout.
Pose.Name: Seated Calf Raise (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Seated Calf Raise Machine or Heavy Barbell on Knees

steps.step: Sit on a seated calf raise machine or bench with a heavy barbell across your knees. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible, keeping your knees bent.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your soleus.
Inhale as you lower your heels down over 3 seconds, feeling a deep stretch. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
The soleus is made up of slow-twitch fibers and responds well to heavy weight, high reps (15-20), and slow tempos.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using hands to push down on knees.
Not going through full range of motion.
Letting heels rest at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and body stable. Use a full range of motion. Do not bounce. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Donkey Calf Raise
Pose.Equipment: Donkey Calf Raise Machine or Partner with Weights on Lower Back

steps.step: Position yourself on a donkey calf raise machine or bend over with a partner sitting on your lower back. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Keep your legs straight (but not locked).
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels below the step over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
The donkey calf raise position allows for heavy loading while maintaining a deep stretch. Keep your hips extended and core braced.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the knees during the raise.
Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and body stable. Use a full range of motion. Do not lock your knee. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Single-Leg Calf Raise
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Achilles tendinitis, ankle injury, balance issues

steps.step: Stand on one leg with the ball of your foot on a step. Hold a dumbbell in the same-side hand (optional). Hold a wall for balance.
Exhale as you rise onto the ball of your foot as high as possible. Squeeze your calf at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your calf.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heel below the step over 3 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps per leg.
Single-leg calf raises help address strength imbalances between legs. Perform the same number of reps on each leg, starting with your weaker side.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning too heavily on the wall.

Pose.safety: Use a squat rack with safety bars. Keep your core braced and back straight. Do not round your lower back. Use a spotter for heavy sets. Warm up thoroughly.
Pose.Name: Barbell Back Squat (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell, Squat Rack, Heavy Plates, Belt (Optional), Spotter
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Quadriceps (Primary), Glutes, Hamstrings, Core, Lower Back
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, severe knee injury, hip impingement

steps.step: Place the barbell across your upper back. Step under, grip wider than shoulders. Unrack with a deep breath and braced core.
Inhale and brace. Push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering with control over 2-3 seconds.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or below. Keep your chest up and weight on mid-foot.
Exhale explosively as you drive through your feet to return to standing. Perform 5 sets of 5 reps.
At expert level, every rep must be perfect. If form breaks, the set is done. Use a belt for sets above 85% of your max. Never sacrifice form for weight.

common_mistakes.mistake: Rounding the lower back at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your torso as upright as possible. The bar will rest on your front deltoids—this may be uncomfortable at first. Keep your elbows high.
Pose.Name: Front Squat
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Wrist mobility issues, thoracic spine tightness, knee injury

steps.step: Rest the barbell on your front deltoids with your elbows high and parallel to the floor. Cross your arms or use a clean grip. Unrack.
Inhale and brace your core. Lower into a squat, keeping your torso as upright as possible.
Lower until your thighs are parallel or below. Keep your elbows high throughout.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 6 reps.
The front squat forces an upright torso, which emphasizes the quads. If your elbows drop, the bar will roll forward. Keep them high.

common_mistakes.mistake: Resting the bar on the collarbone (painful).

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Do not let your hips lift. Control the sled throughout. Use safety stops.
Pose.Name: Hack Squat

steps.step: Position yourself in the hack squat machine with your shoulders under the pads and feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Inhale as you slowly lower the sled, bringing your knees toward your chest. Keep your lower back pressed against the pad.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the platform or below. Do not let your hips lift.
Exhale as you press through your heels to return to the starting position. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Hack squats allow you to load the quads heavily with less spinal stress than back squats. Keep your feet low on the platform for quad emphasis.

common_mistakes.mistake: Lifting hips off the pad at the bottom.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set—have lighter weights ready. Do not rest between drops. Control the weight throughout. Stop if form breaks.
Pose.Name: Leg Extension (Drop Set)
Pose.Equipment: Leg Extension Machine, Multiple Weight Increments

steps.step: Set up three weight increments on the leg extension machine: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Squeeze your quads at the top of each rep.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets are intense. Perform them at the end of your quad workout, not the beginning. The goal is complete muscle fatigue.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not having weights ready for quick transition.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Do not let your front knee travel past your toes. Use a heavy dumbbell in each hand for loading.
Pose.Name: Bulgarian Split Squat (Weighted - Heavy)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury, hip impingement, balance issues

steps.step: Stand 2 feet in front of a bench. Place the top of your back foot on the bench. Hold heavy dumbbells at your sides.
Inhale as you lower your back knee toward the floor, keeping your front heel planted and chest up.
Lower until your front thigh is parallel to the floor or below.
Exhale as you drive through your front heel to return to standing. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps per leg.
This is one of the best quad builders, but it's demanding. Master bodyweight first, then add weight gradually. Keep your torso upright—no leaning forward.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting front knee travel past toes.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Maintain a slight bend in your knees. Do not round your back at any point.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, hamstring tear, sciatica

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a heavy barbell. Keep a slight bend in your knees and core braced.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the bar down your shins. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (typically mid-shin). Do not round your back.
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 5 sets of 5 reps.
At expert weight levels, every rep requires perfect form. If you feel any back rounding, the weight is too heavy. Use a belt for heavy sets.

Pose.safety: Use a pad under your knees. Control the descent—do not drop. This is an advanced exercise. Build up to full ROM over weeks.
Pose.Name: Nordic Curl
Pose.Equipment: Nordic Bench, Pad for Knees, Partner or Anchor

steps.step: Kneel on a pad with your ankles secured. Keep your body straight from knees to head.
Inhale as you slowly lean forward, resisting with your hamstrings. Control the descent over 4-5 seconds.
Lower until your body is parallel to the floor or you can no longer resist.
Push yourself back up with your hands. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps (partial range if needed).
Full Nordic curls are extremely difficult. Most experts still use partial range. Focus on controlling the descent—that's where the strength is built.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting hips bend (breaking at the waist).
Not using a pad under knees.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Control the weight throughout. Stop if form breaks.
Pose.Name: Lying Leg Curl (Drop Set)
Pose.Equipment: Lying Leg Curl Machine, Multiple Weight Increments

steps.step: Set up three weight increments on the lying leg curl machine: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Squeeze your hamstrings at the top of each rep.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets on hamstrings are brutal but effective. Keep your hips pressed into the pad throughout all drops.

Pose.safety: Keep your lower back pressed against the seat. Control the weight throughout. Do not lock your knees at the bottom.
Pose.Name: Seated Leg Curl

steps.step: Sit on the seated leg curl machine with your lower back pressed against the pad. Place your ankles behind the roller pads.
Exhale as you curl the weight toward your glutes over 2 seconds. Squeeze your hamstrings at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your hamstrings as hard as possible.
Inhale as you lower with control over 3 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
The seated position emphasizes the short head of the hamstring. Use a controlled tempo (2-1-3) for maximum muscle fiber recruitment.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your back. Start with light weight to master form.
Pose.Name: Good Morning
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Hamstrings, Glutes, Lower Back (Erector Spinae), Core
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, sciatica, hamstring tear

steps.step: Place a barbell across your upper back (low bar position). Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, slight bend in knees.
Inhale as you push your hips back, bending forward at the waist. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the floor (or as far as hamstring flexibility allows).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Good mornings are an excellent hamstring and lower back exercise, but form is critical. If you feel any back rounding, reduce weight or range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and ribs down. Squeeze your glutes forcefully at the top. Use a pad on the barbell. Do not arch your lower back.
Pose.Name: Barbell Hip Thrust (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell, Bench, Hip Thrust Pad, Belt (Optional)

steps.step: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench. Roll a heavy barbell over your hips. Use a pad.
Exhale as you drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes as hard as possible.
Pause for 3 seconds at the top, squeezing your glutes. Your body should form a straight line.
Inhale as you lower with control over 2 seconds. Perform 5 sets of 5 reps.
At expert level, hip thrusts can be loaded very heavy. Use a belt for support and focus on glute activation—not back extension.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Drive through your heels. Do not round your back. Use a mixed grip for heavy weight.
Pose.Name: Sumo Deadlift (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell with Plates, Deadlift Platform
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Glutes, Adductors (Inner Thighs), Hamstrings, Quadriceps, Lower Back
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, groin pull, knee injury

steps.step: Stand with feet wide (outside shoulder width), toes pointed outward. Grip the bar inside your knees, arms straight.
Inhale and brace your core. Drive through your heels to stand up, keeping your back straight. Push your knees outward.
At the top, squeeze your glutes and stand tall. Do not hyperextend.
Inhale as you lower the bar with control back to the floor. Perform 4 sets of 6 reps.
Sumo deadlifts are a competition lift. Use a belt for heavy sets. Keep your chest up and your knees pushed outward throughout.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with the arms instead of driving with legs.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest up and core braced. Do not let your front knee travel past your toes. Use heavy dumbbells. Control the movement.
Pose.Name: Bulgarian Split Squat (Expert - Heavy)
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Knee injury, hip impingement, balance issues

steps.step: Stand 2 feet in front of a bench. Place the top of your back foot on the bench. Hold heavy dumbbells at your sides.
Inhale as you lower your back knee toward the floor over 3 seconds. Keep your front heel planted and chest up.
Lower until your front thigh is below parallel. Pause for 1 second.
Exhale as you drive through your front heel to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps per leg.
This is a glute-dominant variation. Keep your torso slightly leaned forward to emphasize the glutes over the quads.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting front knee travel past toes.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and back flat. Avoid arching your lower back. Squeeze your glute at the peak. Control the slow return.
Pose.Name: Cable Kickback (Slow Eccentric)

steps.step: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable pulley. Attach to your ankle. Stand facing the machine, holding the frame.
Exhale as you kick your leg straight back over 1 second, keeping a slight bend in your knee. Squeeze your glute.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your glute as hard as possible.
Inhale as you return with control over 4-5 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps per leg.
The slow eccentric (lowering) phase builds glute strength and size. Fight the cable on the way back down—don't let it pull you.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning forward to get more range.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and lower back neutral. Squeeze your glutes at the top of each pump. Do not arch your back.
Pose.Name: Frog Pump

steps.step: Lie on your back with the soles of your feet together and knees dropped out to the sides (frog position). Place your hands on your hips or at your sides.
Exhale as you squeeze your glutes to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Keep your feet together throughout.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your glutes as hard as possible.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 20 reps.
Frog pumps target the upper glutes effectively. Focus on squeezing your glutes, not your lower back. Use a light dumbbell on your hips for added resistance.

common_mistakes.mistake: Arching the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and stand tall. Use a full range of motion. Do not bounce at the bottom. Control the descent. Use a heavy weight.
Pose.Name: Standing Calf Raise (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Standing Calf Raise Machine or Heavy Barbell with Step

steps.step: Stand on a standing calf raise machine or with a heavy barbell across your shoulders. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Squeeze your calves at the top.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels below the step over 3-4 seconds. Perform 5 sets of 12 reps.
Calves can handle high volume and heavy weight. Use a 3-1-3 tempo (3 seconds down, 1 second pause, 3 seconds up) for maximum growth.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your knees bent at a consistent angle. Do not use your hands to push down. Control the weight throughout.
Pose.Name: Seated Calf Raise (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Seated Calf Raise Machine or Heavy Barbell on Knees

steps.step: Sit on a seated calf raise machine or bench with a heavy barbell across your knees. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible, keeping your knees bent.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your soleus.
Inhale as you lower your heels down over 3-4 seconds, feeling a deep stretch. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
The soleus is a slow-twitch dominant muscle. Use higher reps (15-20) with heavy weight and slow tempos for best results.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using hands to push down on knees.
Not going through full range of motion.
Letting heels rest at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and body stable. Use a full range of motion. Do not bounce. Control the descent.
Pose.Name: Donkey Calf Raise (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Donkey Calf Raise Machine or Weighted Partner

steps.step: Position yourself on a donkey calf raise machine or bend over with a partner sitting on your lower back. Place the balls of your feet on a step.
Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet as high as possible. Keep your legs straight (but not locked).
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels below the step over 3-4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
Donkey calf raises allow for the heaviest loading of any calf exercise. Keep your hips extended and lower back neutral throughout.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the knees during the raise.
Not going through full range of motion.

Pose.safety: Keep your core engaged and body stable. Use a full range of motion. Do not lock your knee. Control the descent. Use heavy weight.
Pose.Name: Single-Leg Calf Raise (Weighted - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Dumbbell, Step, Wall for Support

steps.step: Stand on one leg with the ball of your foot on a step. Hold a heavy dumbbell in the same-side hand. Hold a wall for balance.
Exhale as you rise onto the ball of your foot as high as possible. Squeeze your calf at the top.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top, squeezing your calf.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heel below the step over 3-4 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps per leg.
Single-leg calf raises are excellent for correcting strength imbalances. Always start with your weaker leg and match reps on the stronger side.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning too heavily on the wall.

Pose.safety: Keep your legs straight (but not locked) on the leg press. Use a full range of motion. Do not let the weight stack slam at the bottom.
Pose.Name: Calf Press on Leg Press (Heavy - Expert)

steps.step: Sit on the leg press machine. Place the balls of your feet on the bottom edge of the platform, heels hanging off. Keep your legs straight (but not locked).
Exhale as you press the platform away by rising onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your calves.
Inhale as you lower your heels below the platform edge over 3 seconds, feeling a deep stretch. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
The leg press allows for the heaviest calf loading possible. Keep your legs straight—don't use your quads to assist. Use a 3-1-3 tempo.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending and straightening the knees (using quads).
Not going through full range of motion.
Slamming the weight at the bottom.
